mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns do not significantly exceed negative returns following rate cuts (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic strength, driving corporate earnings revisions upward and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicate supply chain bottlenecks that force immediate equity repricing, causing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of triggered observations, or the mechanism fails to predict direction during documented recovery periods (accuracy < 52%).

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a stagnant federal funds rate erodes real yields and prompts market anticipation of aggressive central bank tightening, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases > 0.3% monthly while FEDFUNDS remains unchanged
failure_condition: The combined signal produces positive 20-day returns in > 58% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for term premium movements (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly housing starts figure falling below 500,000 units signals a collapse in residential investment demand that drags down construction sector earnings and produces negative S&P 500 returns over three months.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns exceed zero in > 65% of triggered instances, or the threshold loses predictive power when housing inventory levels are simultaneously rising.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in total nonfarm payrolls directly boost aggregate wage income and consumer demand expectations, triggering immediate algorithmic buying and positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of positive payroll surprise months, or the signal rank correlation drops below 0.01 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and flat real GDP indicates inventory accumulation rather than final demand growth, prompting profit margin compression fears and negative S&P 500 returns over one month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 0.5% while GDPC1 growth remains < 0.2% quarterly
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields positive 20-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the mechanism fails to predict returns when capacity utilization remains above 80%.
